oai_response.prompt:
Here is code in Python 3 programming lanaguge. Translate the following code from Python 3 to C programming lanaguge. Do not output any extra description or tokens other than the translated code. 

from math import gcd

n, m = map(int, input().split())
ans=0
cur=m
g=2
q=m*m
for i in range(2, n+1):
    if gcd(g, i)==1 and g < 10 ** 13:
        g*=i
    cur*=m//g
    cur=cur%998244353
    ans+=q-cur
    q*=m
    q=q%998244353
    ans=ans%998244353
print(ans%998244353)||END-of-SRC||

source_data.prob_desc_input_spec: The only line of the input contains two integers $$$n$$$ and $$$m$$$ ($$$2 \le n \le 3 \cdot 10^5$$$; $$$1 \le m \le 10^{12}$$$).
source_data.source_code:
from math import gcd

n, m = map(int, input().split())
ans=0
cur=m
g=2
q=m*m
for i in range(2, n+1):
    if gcd(g, i)==1 and g < 10 ** 13:
        g*=i
    cur*=m//g
    cur=cur%998244353
    ans+=q-cur
    q*=m
    q=q%998244353
    ans=ans%998244353
print(ans%998244353)
source_data.prob_desc_output_spec: Print one integer — the number of ambiguous arrays $$$a$$$ such that the length of $$$a$$$ is from $$$1$$$ to $$$n$$$ and each $$$a_i$$$ is an integer from $$$1$$$ to $$$m$$$. Since the answer can be very large, print it modulo $$$998244353$$$.
source_data.prob_desc_description: Consider an array $$$a$$$ of length $$$n$$$ with elements numbered from $$$1$$$ to $$$n$$$. It is possible to remove the $$$i$$$-th element of $$$a$$$ if $$$gcd(a_i, i) = 1$$$, where $$$gcd$$$ denotes the greatest common divisor. After an element is removed, the elements to the right are shifted to the left by one position.An array $$$b$$$ with $$$n$$$ integers such that $$$1 \le b_i \le n - i + 1$$$ is a removal sequence for the array $$$a$$$ if it is possible to remove all elements of $$$a$$$, if you remove the $$$b_1$$$-th element, then the $$$b_2$$$-th, ..., then the $$$b_n$$$-th element. For example, let $$$a = [42, 314]$$$:  $$$[1, 1]$$$ is a removal sequence: when you remove the $$$1$$$-st element of the array, the condition $$$gcd(42, 1) = 1$$$ holds, and the array becomes $$$[314]$$$; when you remove the $$$1$$$-st element again, the condition $$$gcd(314, 1) = 1$$$ holds, and the array becomes empty.  $$$[2, 1]$$$ is not a removal sequence: when you try to remove the $$$2$$$-nd element, the condition $$$gcd(314, 2) = 1$$$ is false. An array is ambiguous if it has at least two removal sequences. For example, the array $$$[1, 2, 5]$$$ is ambiguous: it has removal sequences $$$[3, 1, 1]$$$ and $$$[1, 2, 1]$$$. The array $$$[42, 314]$$$ is not ambiguous: the only removal sequence it has is $$$[1, 1]$$$.You are given two integers $$$n$$$ and $$$m$$$. You have to calculate the number of ambiguous arrays $$$a$$$ such that the length of $$$a$$$ is from $$$1$$$ to $$$n$$$ and each $$$a_i$$$ is an integer from $$$1$$$ to $$$m$$$.
